protocol.scope: Public and unauthenticated real-site flows. The benchmark prioritizes operator UX, escalation depth, and recoverability over raw wall-clock speed.
artifacts: per-run analysis fields and manual score placeholders embedded in raw-results.json
reportSections: tasks where both succeed but Silmaril feels worse
tasks where Silmaril needs deeper escalation
tasks where Silmaril shows a product advantage

tasks.title: Search results to asyncio documentation
tasks.description: Start on Python docs search results and navigate into the asyncio reference page.
tasks.successCondition: The asyncio documentation page loads and the main heading contains asyncio.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: search results list to result click
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the search result link is present but cannot be opened within the command budget.

tasks.title: Repository search results to the Playwright repo
tasks.description: Start on GitHub repository search results and open the Microsoft Playwright repository.
tasks.successCondition: The microsoft/playwright repository page loads and exposes the repo name.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: search results list to repo click
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the expected repo result is visible but cannot be activated within budget.

tasks.title: Python.org search results to an asyncio page
tasks.description: Start on Python.org search results and follow an asyncio-related result.
tasks.successCondition: A result page containing asyncio loads in the same tab.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: search results list to relevant result click
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if no asyncio result can be activated cleanly within budget.

tasks.title: Read a Wikipedia article shell
tasks.description: Load a content-heavy Wikipedia article and validate heading plus TOC extraction.
tasks.successCondition: The Pizza article heading and several TOC entries are available.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: open content page and inspect semantic structure
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the article heading loads but core content cannot be extracted within budget.

tasks.title: Read the Fetch API reference shell
tasks.description: Load the MDN Fetch API page and inspect section headings.
tasks.successCondition: The Fetch API heading and multiple section headings are available.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: open content page and inspect section structure
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the page shell loads but section extraction remains ambiguous.

tasks.title: Read the React useState reference shell
tasks.description: Load the React useState reference page and inspect initial paragraphs.
tasks.successCondition: The useState heading and multiple introductory paragraphs are available.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: open content page and inspect content blocks
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the heading is visible but paragraph extraction becomes noisy or empty.

tasks.title: Use the Python.org home-page search form
tasks.description: Enter a query into the Python.org home-page search field and verify the search-results page.
tasks.successCondition: The Python.org search-results page loads for asyncio and exposes asyncio-related links.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: type into public search form and submit
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the search form is visible but submission does not transition cleanly within budget.

tasks.title: Use the Python docs inline search form
tasks.description: Run the inline search from the Python docs home page.
tasks.successCondition: The Python docs search results page loads for asyncio.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: type into inline docs search and submit
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the inline search box accepts text but submission does not route to search results within budget.

tasks.title: Use the Wikipedia main-page search form
tasks.description: Search for Pizza from the Wikipedia main page.
tasks.successCondition: The Pizza article loads in the same tab.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: type into public search form and submit
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the search control is visible but the article route does not complete within budget.

tasks.title: Navigate from useState to useEffect
tasks.description: Use client-side docs navigation from the React useState page to the useEffect page.
tasks.successCondition: The useEffect route loads and the main heading becomes useEffect.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: SPA docs link to SPA docs link target
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the link is present but route change does not complete within budget.

tasks.title: Navigate from the guide intro to Features
tasks.description: Use the Vite docs client-side navigation path from Getting Started to Features.
tasks.successCondition: The Features page loads and the heading contains Features.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: docs link to client-routed page
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the docs link is visible but the route transition does not settle within budget.

tasks.title: Navigate from Astro getting started to Install Astro
tasks.description: Use the Astro docs navigation path from the start page to Install Astro.
tasks.successCondition: The Install Astro page loads and the heading is Install Astro.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: docs landing page to install page
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the install link is visible but route transition does not settle within budget.

tasks.title: Inspect a Behance project feed after scrolling
tasks.description: Open a public Behance search feed, confirm cards exist, and verify the feed remains populated after scrolling.
tasks.successCondition: Project cards are available before and after a below-the-fold scroll.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: feed load, scan, scroll, re-scan
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the feed shell loads but feed cards stay unavailable after the first content scroll.
tasks.silmarilStrengthHypothesis: Content-oriented snapshot guidance should help on nav-heavy feed pages with many competing links.

tasks.title: Inspect a Dribbble search feed
tasks.description: Open a public Dribbble search feed, validate shot links, then scroll and confirm the feed stays populated.
tasks.successCondition: The search feed exposes many shot links and remains populated after scrolling.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: feed load, scan, scroll, re-scan
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the feed shell loads but shot links never become available within budget.

tasks.title: Confirm search-feed growth after scroll
tasks.description: Open a public Flickr search feed, record the initial visible photo-link count, then scroll until a larger visible set is loaded.
tasks.successCondition: At least 120 visible photo links are available after scrolling below the fold.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: feed load, baseline count, scroll, count growth
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the initial feed count is available but no new links appear after one major scroll window.

tasks.title: Open and close the Astro DocSearch modal
tasks.description: Trigger the Astro search modal, validate the input, then close it cleanly.
tasks.successCondition: The DocSearch modal appears with a search input, then disappears after cancel.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: search trigger to modal to close
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the search trigger is present but the modal shell does not appear within budget.

tasks.title: Open the Next.js directory selector
tasks.description: Open the directory selector on Next.js docs and verify the visible menu options.
tasks.successCondition: The directory selector opens and exposes at least two visible options.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: menu trigger to menu content
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the selector button is present but a clean visible-open action is not possible within budget.
tasks.docsVsRealityNote: Next.js docs expose duplicate hidden and visible controls. In practice, visible ref selection is more reliable than raw href or aria-label CSS matching.
tasks.silmarilStrengthHypothesis: Snapshot refs should make visible-control selection clearer than raw selector guessing on duplicated docs chrome.

tasks.title: Open the Next.js version selector
tasks.description: Open the version selector on Next.js docs and verify that multiple versions are available.
tasks.successCondition: The version selector opens and exposes at least two version options.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: menu trigger to menu content
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the selector button is present but a clean visible-open action is not possible within budget.
tasks.docsVsRealityNote: Version controls on Next.js docs also appear in duplicated chrome. Snapshot-driven visible-target resolution is more reliable than first-match CSS.
tasks.silmarilStrengthHypothesis: Snapshot refs should help separate the visible version button from hidden duplicates.

tasks.title: Load a public chess analysis board
tasks.description: Validate that the Lichess analysis board shell and board pieces are available.
tasks.successCondition: The chessboard renders and at least 30 pieces are queryable in the board DOM.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: open custom-rendered app and validate rendered state
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the board shell loads but no piece nodes become available within budget.
tasks.silmarilStrengthHypothesis: Silmaril should do relatively well on runtime-facing DOM inspection for custom-rendered app shells.

tasks.title: Zoom in on a public map
tasks.description: Use the public OpenStreetMap zoom-in control and confirm that the hash-based zoom level changes.
tasks.successCondition: The URL hash changes from map zoom level 5 to zoom level 6.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: click custom map control and verify app state
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the zoom control is present but state does not change after a clean click.

tasks.title: Load the Desmos calculator shell
tasks.description: Validate that the Desmos calculator app shell, zoom controls, and many editor textareas are present.
tasks.successCondition: The calculator shell loads and exposes the zoom button plus many editor textareas.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: open custom-rendered app and inspect runtime shell
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the shell loads but editor controls remain unavailable within budget.
tasks.silmarilStrengthHypothesis: Silmaril should be competitive when the task favors inspecting live app shell state over semantic document content.

tasks.title: Extract a heading, use it in another tab, then return
tasks.description: Read the React page title, use it in a second-tab GitHub search, then switch back and re-verify the original state.
tasks.successCondition: A GitHub search tab is opened with the extracted term, then the original React tab is re-validated.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: read in tab A, use in tab B, switch back to tab A
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the second tab opens with the right query but returning to the source tab does not preserve the source heading.
tasks.silmarilStrengthHypothesis: Explicit target handling should be one of Silmaril's clearest advantages over generic tab-local interaction loops.

tasks.title: Carry a Wikipedia title into another search tab
tasks.description: Read a Wikipedia article title, open a Python.org search tab with that value, then return to the source tab.
tasks.successCondition: The second tab opens with the extracted query and the original source tab still exposes Pizza.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: read in tab A, use in tab B, switch back to tab A
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the second tab opens but returning to the source tab loses the source heading.
tasks.silmarilStrengthHypothesis: Target pinning and explicit tab routing should reduce ambiguity in cross-tab reference tasks.

tasks.title: Carry an MDN page title into a Wikipedia search tab
tasks.description: Read the MDN Fetch API title, open a Wikipedia search tab with that term, then return to the MDN source tab.
tasks.successCondition: The second tab opens a Wikipedia search URL for the extracted term and the MDN source tab remains intact.
tasks.expectedPrimaryInteractionPattern: read in tab A, use in tab B, switch back to tab A
tasks.stopCondition: Stop if the second tab opens but the original MDN source heading cannot be recovered afterward.
tasks.silmarilStrengthHypothesis: Silmaril should benefit from explicit tab identity on multi-tab reference work where URL-derived selectors are secondary.
